description: Phase 1 tokenizer-parity input pack for the experimental DiffusionGemma lane: hello, the established short/trailing-spaces/special-chars/longer/multiline edge texts, two tokenizer-specific edges (newline runs, exotic bytes), and five chat-template cases. Raw cases tokenize with add_special=true / parse_special=false; chat cases render the model's own chat template (add_generation_prompt=true) and tokenize with parse_special=true, mirroring the reference diffusion CLI. This pack makes no support claim for any row.

cases.text: Reply with the word camelid and preserve this prompt's trailing spaces.
Echo a compact summary of these symbols: []{}<>|~^`\ and emoji alpaca 🦙.
In two short sentences, explain why deterministic token parity matters for a local GGUF inference backend under a strict support contract.

Line one asks for a tiny checklist.

  Line two is indented and mentions TinyLlama Q8_0.
Line three asks for exactly three terse bullets answering all of the above.
Z̢alg̕o text, combining marks: é (é), ñ, 中文, ★, 🦙🦙, tab	end

messages.content: Keep the response concise and deterministic.

Line one asks for a tiny checklist.

  Line two is indented.
Line three asks for exactly three terse bullets answering all of the above.
Echo a compact summary of these symbols: []{}<>|~^`\ and emoji alpaca 🦙.
What is the capital of France?
Reply with the word camelid and preserve this prompt's trailing spaces.
